Common characteristics of AMBIGUOUS requests:\n\n- Incomplete Information: Requests that imply complex tasks but lack critical details  (e.g., "Redesign this logo" without specifying design requirements).\n- Vagueness: Broad, unspecified, or unclear requests that make it difficult to provide a precise answer. \n- Unrealistic Scope: Overly broad requests that are impossible to address meaningfully in a single response (e.g., "Tell me everything about...").\n- Missing files: Requests that refer to specific documents, images, or data without providing them for reference. 001|# Product Requirement Document: student_evaluation_automation
002|
003|## 1. Language & Project Info
004|- **Language:** English
005|- **Programming Language:** Python
006|- **Project Name:** student_evaluation_automation
007|
008|### Restated Requirements
009|Develop a Python program to automate the evaluation of incoming students by:
010|- Integrating data from 3 MachForms into Google Sheets
011|- Compiling the data into a master record
012|- Using the OpenAI API to generate personalized academic pathways
013|- Filling a branded Google Docs template with the pathway
014|- Exporting the result as a PDF
015|- Emailing the PDF to the student
016|- Storing the PDF in Salesforce
017|- Providing a tracking interface for admins
018|
019|## 2. Product Definition
020|### Product Goals
021|1. Seamlessly automate the student evaluation workflow from data collection to personalized academic pathway delivery.
022|2. Ensure secure, accurate, and efficient integration with MachForms, Google Sheets, Google Docs, Salesforce, and email systems.
023|3. Provide a robust tracking and management interface for administrators to monitor student progress and document status.
024|
025|### User Stories
026|- As an admissions officer, I want to automatically compile student data from multiple forms so that I can save time and reduce manual errors.
027|- As a student, I want to receive a personalized academic pathway PDF via email so that I understand my recommended courses and next steps.
028|- As an admin, I want to track the status of each student’s evaluation and document delivery so that I can ensure timely follow-up.
029|- As a system integrator, I want the solution to securely store records in Salesforce so that all student documentation is centralized.
030|- As a program manager, I want the branded Google Docs template to be filled automatically so that all communications maintain a professional appearance.
031|### Competitive Analysis
032|
033|| Product Name                | Pros                                                      | Cons                                                      |
034||-----------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|
035|| Slate by Technolutions      | Comprehensive admissions CRM, strong integrations         | Expensive, complex setup                                  |
036|| Ellucian CRM Recruit        | Deep SIS integration, robust reporting                    | Less customizable, slower support                         |
037|| Salesforce Education Cloud  | Highly customizable, strong automation, scalable          | High cost, requires expert setup                          |
038|| Kira Talent                 | Video assessment, workflow automation                     | Limited to interview/evaluation, not full workflow        |
039|| FormAssembly + Salesforce   | Flexible form builder, native Salesforce integration       | Manual pathway generation, less automation                |
040|| Google Workspace + Apps Script | Low cost, flexible, leverages Google ecosystem         | Requires custom scripting, limited out-of-box features    |
041|| Target Product              | End-to-end automation, AI-driven personalization, seamless integration | New product, requires initial configuration               |

001|# Product Requirement Document: student_evaluation_automation
002|
003|## 1. Language & Project Info
004|- **Language:** English
005|- **Programming Language:** Python
006|- **Project Name:** student_evaluation_automation
007|
008|### Restated Requirements
009|Develop a Python program to automate the evaluation of incoming students by:
010|- Integrating data from 3 MachForms into Google Sheets
011|- Compiling the data into a master record
012|- Using the OpenAI API to generate personalized academic pathways
013|- Filling a branded Google Docs template with the pathway
014|- Exporting the result as a PDF
015|- Emailing the PDF to the student
016|- Storing the PDF in Salesforce
017|- Providing a tracking interface for admins
018|
019|## 2. Product Definition
020|### Product Goals
021|1. Seamlessly automate the student evaluation workflow from data collection to personalized academic pathway delivery.
022|2. Ensure secure, accurate, and efficient integration with MachForms, Google Sheets, Google Docs, Salesforce, and email systems.
023|3. Provide a robust tracking and management interface for administrators to monitor student progress and document status.
024|
025|### User Stories
026|- As an admissions officer, I want to automatically compile student data from multiple forms so that I can save time and reduce manual errors.
027|- As a student, I want to receive a personalized academic pathway PDF via email so that I understand my recommended courses and next steps.
028|- As an admin, I want to track the status of each student’s evaluation and document delivery so that I can ensure timely follow-up.
029|- As a system integrator, I want the solution to securely store records in Salesforce so that all student documentation is centralized.
030|- As a program manager, I want the branded Google Docs template to be filled automatically so that all communications maintain a professional appearance.
031|### Competitive Analysis
032|
033|| Product Name                | Pros                                                      | Cons                                                      |
034||-----------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|
035|| Slate by Technolutions      | Comprehensive admissions CRM, strong integrations         | Expensive, complex setup                                  |
036|| Ellucian CRM Recruit        | Deep SIS integration, robust reporting                    | Less customizable, slower support                         |
037|| Salesforce Education Cloud  | Highly customizable, strong automation, scalable          | High cost, requires expert setup                          |
038|| Kira Talent                 | Video assessment, workflow automation                     | Limited to interview/evaluation, not full workflow        |
039|| FormAssembly + Salesforce   | Flexible form builder, native Salesforce integration       | Manual pathway generation, less automation                |
040|| Google Workspace + Apps Script | Low cost, flexible, leverages Google ecosystem         | Requires custom scripting, limited out-of-box features    |
041|| Target Product              | End-to-end automation, AI-driven personalization, seamless integration | New product, requires initial configuration               |
042|
043|```mermaid
044|quadrantChart
045|    title "Student Evaluation Automation Competitive Positioning"
046|    x-axis "Low Automation" --> "High Automation"
047|    y-axis "Low Personalization" --> "High Personalization"
048|    quadrant-1 "Innovate"
049|    quadrant-2 "Enhance Personalization"
050|    quadrant-3 "Increase Automation"
051|    quadrant-4 "Market Leaders"
052|    "Slate": [0.7, 0.6]
053|    "Ellucian": [0.6, 0.5]
054|    "Salesforce Ed Cloud": [0.8, 0.7]
055|    "Kira Talent": [0.5, 0.4]
056|    "FormAssembly+SF": [0.4, 0.3]
057|    "Google Workspace+Script": [0.3, 0.2]
058|    "Target Product": [0.9, 0.9]
059|```
060|## 3. Technical Specifications
061|
062|### Requirements Analysis
063|- Integration with MachForms: Must securely fetch and parse data from three distinct MachForms.
064|- Google Sheets Sync: Must aggregate and update student data in a master Google Sheet in real time.
065|- OpenAI API: Must generate personalized academic pathways based on student data and program requirements.
066|- Google Docs Automation: Must fill a branded template with pathway details and export as PDF.
067|- Email Delivery: Must send the PDF to the student’s email address automatically.
068|- Salesforce Storage: Must upload the PDF and student record to Salesforce, linking to the correct contact.
069|- Admin Tracking Interface: Must provide a dashboard for admins to monitor evaluation status, document delivery, and Salesforce sync.
070|- Security: Must comply with FERPA and institutional data privacy standards.
071|
072|### Requirements Pool
073|- **P0 (Must-have):**
074|  - MachForms integration
075|  - Google Sheets master record
076|  - OpenAI API pathway generation
077|  - Google Docs template automation
078|  - PDF export and email delivery
079|  - Salesforce record storage
080|  - Admin tracking dashboard
081|- **P1 (Should-have):**
082|  - Error handling and retry logic
083|  - Audit logs for all actions
084|  - Customizable email templates
085|- **P2 (Nice-to-have):**
086|  - Mobile-friendly admin interface
087|  - Analytics on student pathway trends
088|  - Multi-language support
089|
090|### UI Design Draft
091|- **Admin Dashboard:**
092|  - Student list with status indicators (data received, pathway generated, PDF sent, Salesforce stored)
093|  - Search and filter by name, status, date
094|  - Detail view for each student: form data, pathway, document links
095|  - Manual override and re-send options
096|- **Student Email:**
097|  - Branded message with attached PDF
098|  - Link to contact admissions for questions
099|
100|### Open Questions
101|- What is the expected volume of incoming students per cycle?
102|- Are there specific data fields or formats required from MachForms?
103|- Should admins be able to edit pathways before sending?
104|- What branding elements are required for the Google Docs template?
105|- What Salesforce object(s) should the PDF be attached to?

DocsClient\n    EmailSender <.. StudentRecord\n    SalesforceClient <.. StudentRecord\n    Logger <.. AdminDashboard\n```\n\n## Program call flow:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant MF as MachFormClient\n    participant SC as SheetsClient\n    participant OA as OpenAIClient\n    participant DC as DocsClient\n    participant PE as PDFExporter\n    participant ES as EmailSender\n    participant SF as SalesforceClient\n    participant AD as AdminDashboard\n    participant SR as StudentRecord\n    participant LG as Logger\n    MF->>MF: fetch_form_data(form_id)\n    MF-->>SR: return machform_data\n    SR->>SC: update_master_sheet(student_records)\n    SC-->>SR: get_student_records()\n    SR->>OA: generate_pathway(student_data)\n    OA-->>SR: return academic_pathway\n    SR->>DC: fill_template(student)\n    DC-->>SR: return doc_id\n    SR->>DC: export_pdf(doc_id)\n    DC-->>SR: return pdf_path\n    SR->>PE: export(doc_path)\n    PE-->>SR: return pdf_path\n    SR->>ES: send_email(to, subject, body, attachment)\n    ES-->>SR: email sent\n    SR->>SF: upload_document(student, pdf_path)\n    SF-->>SR: return salesforce_id\n    AD->>SR: get_status(student_id)\n    AD->>SR: list_students(status)\n    AD->>SR: manual_resend(student_id)\n    LG->>AD: log(action, details)\n```\n\n## Anything UNCLEAR\n\n- MachForm API details: Is there a REST API or only webhooks/exports?\n- Exact data fields/formats from MachForms?\n- Should admins be able to edit pathways before sending?\n- Branding requirements for Google Docs template?\n- Salesforce object for PDF attachment?\n- Expected student volume per cycle?\n'}}]

AdminDashboard\\n092|```\\n093|\\n094|## Program call flow:\\n095|\\n096|```mermaid\\n097|sequenceDiagram\\n098|    participant MF as MachFormClient\\n099|    participant SC as SheetsClient\\n100|    participant OA as OpenAIClient\\n101|    participant DC as DocsClient\\n102|    participant PE as PDFExporter\\n103|    participant ES as EmailSender\\n104|    participant SF as SalesforceClient\\n105|    participant AD as AdminDashboard\\n106|    participant SR as StudentRecord\\n107|    participant LG as Logger\\n108|    MF->>MF: fetch_form_data(form_id)\\n109|    MF-->>SR: return machform_data\\n110|    SR->>SC: update_master_sheet(student_records)\\n111|    SC-->>SR: get_student_records()\\n112|    SR->>OA: generate_pathway(student_data)\\n113|    OA-->>SR: return academic_pathway\\n114|    SR->>DC: fill_template(student)\\n115|    DC-->>SR: return doc_id\\n116|    SR->>DC: export_pdf(doc_id)\\n117|    DC-->>SR: return pdf_path\\n118|    SR->>PE: export(doc_path)\\n119|    PE-->>SR: return pdf_path\\n120|    SR->>ES: send_email(to, subject, body, attachment)\\n121|    ES-->>SR: email sent\\n122|    SR->>SF: upload_document(student, pdf_path)\\n123|    SF-->>SR: return salesforce_id\\n124|    AD->>SR: get_status(student_id)\\n125|    AD->>SR: list_students(status)\\n126|    AD->>SR: manual_resend(student_id)\\n127|    LG->>AD: log(action, details)\\n128|```\\n129|\\n130|## Anything UNCLEAR\\n131|\\n132|- MachForm API details: Is there a REST API or only webhooks/exports?\\n133|- Exact data fields/formats from from typing import Dict, Optional


class StudentRecord:
    """
    Represents a student's evaluation record, including form data, generated pathway,
    document links, and status tracking.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        student_id: str,
        name: str,
        email: str,
        machform_data: Dict[str, Dict],
        academic_pathway: Optional[str] = None,
        pdf_path: Optional[str] = None,
        salesforce_id: Optional[str] = None,
        status: str = "pending"
    ):
        """
        Initialize a StudentRecord.

        Args:
            student_id (str): Unique identifier for the student.
            name (str): Student's full name.
            email (str): Student's email address.
            machform_data (Dict[str, Dict]): Data from each MachForm, keyed by form ID or name.
            academic_pathway (Optional[str]): Generated academic pathway text.
            pdf_path (Optional[str]): Path to the generated PDF document.
            salesforce_id (Optional[str]): Salesforce record/document ID.
            status (str): Current processing status.
        """
        self.student_id = student_id
        self.name = name
        self.email = email
        self.machform_data = machform_data  # e.g., {'form1': {...}, 'form2': {...}, 'form3': {...}}
        self.academic_pathway = academic_pathway
        self.pdf_path = pdf_path
        self.salesforce_id = salesforce_id
        self.status = status  # e.g., 'pending', 'data_collected', 'pathway_generated', 'pdf_sent', 'sf_uploaded', 'completed', 'error'

    def to_dict(self) -> Dict:
        """
        Serialize the student record to a dictionary for storage or transmission.

        Returns:
            Dict: Dictionary representation of the student record.
        """
        return {
            "student_id": self.student_id,
            "name": self.name,
            "email": self.email,
            "machform_data": self.machform_data,
            "academic_pathway": self.academic_pathway,
            "pdf_path": self.pdf_path,
            "salesforce_id": self.salesforce_id,
            "status": self.status,
        }

    @classmethod
    def from_dict(cls, data: Dict) -> "StudentRecord":
        """
        Create a StudentRecord from a dictionary.

        Args:
            data (Dict): Dictionary containing student record data.

        Returns:
            StudentRecord: The constructed StudentRecord object.
        """
        return cls(
            student_id=data.get("student_id", ""),
            name=data.get("name", ""),
            email=data.get("email", ""),
            machform_data=data.get("machform_data", {}),
            academic_pathway=data.get("academic_pathway"),
            pdf_path=data.get("pdf_path"),
            salesforce_id=data.get("salesforce_id"),
            status=data.get("status", "pending"),
        )

3. Technical Specifications\\n061|\\n062|### Requirements Analysis\\n063|- Integration with MachForms: Must securely fetch and parse data from three distinct MachForms.\\n064|- Google Sheets Sync: Must aggregate and update student data in a master Google Sheet in real time.\\n065|- OpenAI API: Must generate personalized academic pathways based on student data and program requirements.\\n066|- Google Docs Automation: Must fill a branded template with pathway details and export as PDF.\\n067|- Email Delivery: Must send the PDF to the student’s email address automatically.\\n068|- Salesforce Storage: Must upload the PDF and student record to Salesforce, linking to the correct contact.\\n069|- Admin Tracking Interface: Must provide a dashboard for admins to monitor evaluation status, document delivery, and Salesforce sync.\\n070|- Security: Must comply with FERPA and institutional data privacy standards.\\n071|\\n072|### Requirements Pool\\n073|- **P0 (Must-have):**\\n074|  - Mobile-friendly admin interface\\n087|  - Analytics on student pathway trends\\n088|  - Multi-language support\\n089|\\n090|### UI Design Draft\\n091|- **Admin Dashboard:**\\n092|  - Student list with status indicators (data received, pathway generated, PDF sent, Salesforce stored)\\n093|  - AdminDashboard\\n084|    MachFormClient <.. StudentRecord\\n085|    SheetsClient <.. StudentRecord\\n086|    OpenAIClient <.. StudentRecord\\n087|    DocsClient <.. StudentRecord\\n088|    PDFExporter <.. DocsClient\\n089|    EmailSender <.. StudentRecord\\n090|    SalesforceClient <.. StudentRecord\\n091|    Logger <.. AdminDashboard\\n092|```\\n093|\\n094|## Program call flow:\\n095|\\n096|```mermaid\\n097|sequenceDiagram\\n098|    participant MF as MachFormClient\\n099|    participant SC as SheetsClient\\n100|    participant OA as OpenAIClient\\n101|    participant DC as DocsClient\\n102|    participant PE as PDFExporter\\n103|    participant ES as EmailSender\\n104|    participant SF as SalesforceClient\\n105|    participant AD as AdminDashboard\\n106|    participant SR as StudentRecord\\n107|    participant LG as Logger\\n108|    MF->>MF: fetch_form_data(form_id)\\n109|    MF-->>SR: return machform_data\\n110|    SR->>SC: update_master_sheet(student_records)\\n111|    SC-->>SR: get_student_records()\\n112|    SR->>OA: generate_pathway(student_data)\\n113|    OA-->>SR: return academic_pathway\\n114|    SR->>DC: fill_template(student)\\n115|    DC-->>SR: return doc_id\\n116|    SR->>DC: export_pdf(doc_id)\\n117|    DC-->>SR: return pdf_path\\n118|    SR->>PE: export(doc_path)\\n119|    PE-->>SR: return pdf_path\\n120|    SR->>ES: send_email(to, subject, body, attachment)\\n121|    ES-->>SR: email sent\\n122|    SR->>SF: upload_document(student, pdf_path)\\n123|    SF-->>SR: return salesforce_id\\n124|    AD->>SR: get_status(student_id)\\n125|    AD->>SR: list_students(status)\\n126|    AD->>SR: manual_resend(student_id)\\n127|    LG->>AD: log(action, details)\\n128|```\\n129|\\n130|## Anything UNCLEAR\\n131|\\n132|- MachForm API details: Is there a REST API or only webhooks/exports?\\n133|- Exact data fields/formats from Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/student_evaluation_automation/machform_client.py has been successfully created, with content:
import requests
from typing import List, Dict, Optional
import logging


class MachFormClient:
    """
    Client to fetch and parse data from MachForms.
    """

    def __init__(self, base_urls: Dict[str, str], api_keys: Dict[str, str], timeout: int = 10):
        """
        Initialize the MachFormClient.

        Args:
            base_urls (Dict[str, str]): Mapping of form_id to MachForm API endpoint URLs.
            api_keys (Dict[str, str]): Mapping of form_id to API keys for authentication.
            timeout (int): Timeout for HTTP requests in seconds.
        """
        self.base_urls = base_urls
        self.api_keys = api_keys
        self.timeout = timeout
        self.logger = logging.getLogger("MachFormClient")

    def fetch_form_data(self, form_id: str) -> List[Dict]:
        """
        Fetch and parse data from a MachForm.

        Args:
            form_id (str): The MachForm ID to fetch data from.

        Returns:
            List[Dict]: List of submission data dictionaries.
        """
        if form_id not in self.base_urls or form_id not in self.api_keys:
            self.logger.error(f"Form ID '{form_id}' not configured.")
            raise ValueError(f"Form ID '{form_id}' not configured.")

        url = self.base_urls[form_id]
        api_key = self.api_keys[form_id]

        headers = {
            "Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}",
            "Accept": "application/json"
        }

        try:
            response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=self.timeout)
            response.raise_for_status()
            data = response.json()
            submissions = self._parse_response(data)
            self.logger.info(f"Fetched {len(submissions)} submissions from form {form_id}.")
            return submissions
        except requests.RequestException as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Error fetching data from MachForm {form_id}: {e}")
            return []
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Unexpected error parsing MachForm {form_id}: {e}")
            return []

    def _parse_response(self, data: dict) -> List[Dict]:
        """
        Parse the MachForm API response into a list of submission dicts.

        Args:
            data (dict): Raw response data from MachForm API.

        Returns:
            List[Dict]: List of parsed submissions.
        """
        # MachForm API responses may vary; adjust parsing as needed.
        # Assume 'entries' is the key for submissions.
        if "entries" in data and isinstance(data["entries"], list):
            return data["entries"]
        elif isinstance(data, list):
            return data
        else:
            # Try to find the first list in the response
            for v in data.values():
                if isinstance(v, list):
                    return v
        return []

    def fetch_all_forms(self, form_ids: Optional[List[str]] = None) -> Dict[str, List[Dict]]:
        """
        Fetch data from all configured MachForms or a subset.

        Args:
            form_ids (Optional[List[str]]): List of form IDs to fetch. If None, fetch all.

        Returns:
            Dict[str, List[Dict]]: Mapping of form_id to list of submissions.
        """
        results = {}
        ids = form_ids if form_ids is not None else list(self.base_urls.keys())
        for form_id in ids:
            results[form_id] = self.fetch_form_data(form_id)
        return results

AdminDashboard\\n092|```\\n093|\\n094|## Program call flow:\\n095|\\n096|```mermaid\\n097|sequenceDiagram\\n098|    participant MF as MachFormClient\\n099|    participant SC as SheetsClient\\n100|    participant OA as OpenAIClient\\n101|    participant DC as DocsClient\\n102|    participant PE as PDFExporter\\n103|    participant ES as EmailSender\\n104|    participant SF as SalesforceClient\\n105|    participant AD as AdminDashboard\\n106|    participant SR as StudentRecord\\n107|    participant LG as Logger\\n108|    MF->>MF: fetch_form_data(form_id)\\n109|    MF-->>SR: return machform_data\\n110|    SR->>SC: update_master_sheet(student_records)\\n111|    SC-->>SR: get_student_records()\\n112|    SR->>OA: generate_pathway(student_data)\\n113|    OA-->>SR: return academic_pathway\\n114|    SR->>DC: fill_template(student)\\n115|    DC-->>SR: return doc_id\\n116|    SR->>DC: export_pdf(doc_id)\\n117|    DC-->>SR: return pdf_path\\n118|    SR->>PE: export(doc_path)\\n119|    PE-->>SR: return pdf_path\\n120|    SR->>ES: send_email(to, subject, body, attachment)\\n121|    ES-->>SR: email sent\\n122|    SR->>SF: upload_document(student, pdf_path)\\n123|    SF-->>SR: return salesforce_id\\n124|    AD->>SR: get_status(student_id)\\n125|    AD->>SR: list_students(status)\\n126|    AD->>SR: manual_resend(student_id)\\n127|    LG->>AD: log(action, details)\\n128|```\\n129|\\n130|## Anything UNCLEAR\\n131|\\n132|- MachForm API details: Is there a REST API or only webhooks/exports?\\n133|- Exact data fields/formats from timeout: int = 10):\n        """\n        Initialize the MachFormClient.\n\n        Args:\n            base_urls (Dict[str, str]): Mapping of form_id to MachForm API endpoint URLs.\n            api_keys (Dict[str, str]): Mapping of form_id to API keys for authentication.\n            timeout (int): Timeout for HTTP requests in seconds.\n        """\n        self.base_urls = base_urls\n        self.api_keys = api_keys\n        self.timeout = timeout\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("MachFormClient")\n\n    def fetch_form_data(self, form_id: str) -> List[Dict]:\n        """\n        Fetch and parse data from a MachForm.\n\n        Args:\n            form_id (str): The MachForm ID to fetch data from.\n\n        Returns:\n            List[Dict]: List of submission data dictionaries.\n        """\n        if form_id not in self.base_urls or form_id not in self.api_keys:\n            self.logger.error(f"Form ID \'{form_id}\' not configured.")\n            raise ValueError(f"Form ID \'{form_id}\' not configured.")\n\n        url = self.base_urls[form_id]\n        api_key = self.api_keys[form_id]\n\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}",\n            "Accept": "application/json"\n        }\n\n        try:\n            response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=self.timeout)\n            response.raise_for_status()\n            data = response.json()\n            submissions = self._parse_response(data)\n            self.logger.info(f"Fetched {len(submissions)} submissions from form {form_id}.")\n            return submissions\n        except requests.RequestException as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Error fetching data from MachForm {form_id}: {e}")\n            return []\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Unexpected error parsing MachForm {form_id}: {e}")\n            return []\n\n    def _parse_response(self, data: dict) -> List[Dict]:\n        """\n        Parse the MachForm API response into a list of submission dicts.\n\n        Args:\n            data (dict): Raw response data from MachForm API.\n\n        Returns:\n            List[Dict]: List of parsed submissions.\n        """\n        # MachForm API responses may vary; adjust parsing as needed.\n        # Assume \'entries\' is the key for submissions.\n        if "entries" in data and isinstance(data["entries"], list):\n            return data["entries"]\n        elif isinstance(data, list):\n            return data\n        else:\n            # Try to find the first list in the response\n            for v in data.values():\n                if isinstance(v, list):\n                    return v\n        return []\n\n    def fetch_all_forms(self, form_ids: form IDs to fetch. import os
import logging
from typing import List
from models import StudentRecord
import gspread
from google.oauth2.service_account import Credentials

class SheetsClient:
    """
    Handles aggregation and update of student data in a master Google Sheet.
    """

    def __init__(self, creds_path: str, sheet_name: str):
        """
        Initialize the SheetsClient.

        Args:
            creds_path (str): Path to the Google service account credentials JSON file.
            sheet_name (str): Name of the master Google Sheet.
        """
        self.logger = logging.getLogger("SheetsClient")
        self.creds_path = creds_path
        self.sheet_name = sheet_name
        self.scope = [
            "https://spreadsheets.google.com/feeds",
            "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive",
            "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/spreadsheets"
        ]
        self.gc = self._authorize()
        self.sheet = self._open_sheet()

    def _authorize(self):
        """
        Authorize with Google Sheets API using service account credentials.

        Returns:
            gspread.Client: Authorized gspread client.
        """
        creds = Credentials.from_service_account_file(self.creds_path, scopes=self.scope)
        gc = gspread.authorize(creds)
        return gc

    def _open_sheet(self):
        """
        Open the master Google Sheet.

        Returns:
            gspread.Spreadsheet: The opened spreadsheet object.
        """
        try:
            sheet = self.gc.open(self.sheet_name)
            self.logger.info(f"Opened Google Sheet: {self.sheet_name}")
            return sheet
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Failed to open Google Sheet '{self.sheet_name}': {e}")
            raise

    def update_master_sheet(self, student_records: List[StudentRecord]):
        """
        Update the master Google Sheet with the provided student records.

        Args:
            student_records (List[StudentRecord]): List of student records to update.
        """
        worksheet = self.sheet.sheet1
        # Prepare header
        header = [
            "student_id", "name", "email", "machform_data", "academic_pathway",
            "pdf_path", "salesforce_id", "status"
        ]
        # Prepare rows
        rows = [header]
        for record in student_records:
            row = [
                record.student_id,
                record.name,
                record.email,
                str(record.machform_data),
                record.academic_pathway if record.academic_pathway else "",
                record.pdf_path if record.pdf_path else "",
                record.salesforce_id if record.salesforce_id else "",
                record.status
            ]
            rows.append(row)
        try:
            worksheet.clear()
            worksheet.append_rows(rows)
            self.logger.info(f"Updated master sheet with {len(student_records)} student records.")
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Failed to update master sheet: {e}")
            raise

    def get_student_records(self) -> List[StudentRecord]:
        """
        Retrieve student records from the master Google Sheet.

        Returns:
            List[StudentRecord]: List of StudentRecord objects.
        """
        worksheet = self.sheet.sheet1
        try:
            all_values = worksheet.get_all_values()
            if not all_values or len(all_values) < 2:
                return []
            header = all_values[0]
            records = []
            for row in all_values[1:]:
                data = dict(zip(header, row))
                # Convert machform_data from string to dict
                try:
                    machform_data = eval(data.get("machform_data", "{}"))
                except Exception:
                    machform_data = {}
                record = StudentRecord(
                    student_id=data.get("student_id", ""),
                    name=data.get("name", ""),
                    email=data.get("email", ""),
                    machform_data=machform_data,
                    academic_pathway=data.get("academic_pathway", ""),
                    pdf_path=data.get("pdf_path", ""),
                    salesforce_id=data.get("salesforce_id", ""),
                    status=data.get("status", "pending")
                )
                records.append(record)
            self.logger.info(f"Retrieved {len(records)} student records from master sheet.")
            return records
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Failed to retrieve student records: {e}")
            return []

Technical Specifications\\n061|\\n062|### Requirements Analysis\\n063|- Integration with MachForms: Must securely fetch and parse data from three distinct MachForms.\\n064|- Google Sheets Sync: Must aggregate and update student data in a master Google Sheet in real time.\\n065|- OpenAI API: Must generate personalized academic pathways based on student data and program requirements.\\n066|- Google Docs Automation: Must fill a branded template with pathway details and export as PDF.\\n067|- Email Delivery: Must send the PDF to the student’s email address automatically.\\n068|- Salesforce Storage: Must upload the PDF and student record to Salesforce, linking to the correct contact.\\n069|- Admin Tracking Interface: Must provide a dashboard for admins to monitor evaluation status, document delivery, and Salesforce sync.\\n070|- Security: Must comply with FERPA and institutional data privacy standards.\\n071|\\n072|### Requirements Pool\\n073|- **P0 (Must-have):**\\n074|  - Optional\nimport logging\n\n\nclass MachFormClient:\n    """\n    Client to fetch and parse data from MachForms.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, base_urls: Dict[str, str], api_keys: Dict[str, str], timeout: int = 10):\n        """\n        Initialize the MachFormClient.\n\n        Args:\n            base_urls (Dict[str, str]): Mapping of form_id to MachForm API endpoint URLs.\n            api_keys (Dict[str, str]): Mapping of form_id to API keys for authentication.\n            timeout (int): Timeout for HTTP requests in seconds.\n        """\n        self.base_urls = base_urls\n        self.api_keys = api_keys\n        self.timeout = timeout\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("MachFormClient")\n\n    def fetch_form_data(self, form_id: str) -> List[Dict]:\n        """\n        Fetch and parse data from a MachForm.\n\n        Args:\n            form_id (str): The MachForm ID to fetch data from.\n\n        Returns:\n            List[Dict]: List of submission data dictionaries.\n        """\n        if form_id not in self.base_urls or form_id not in self.api_keys:\n            self.logger.error(f"Form ID \'{form_id}\' not configured.")\n            raise ValueError(f"Form ID \'{form_id}\' not configured.")\n\n        url = self.base_urls[form_id]\n        api_key = self.api_keys[form_id]\n\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}",\n            "Accept": "application/json"\n        }\n\n        try:\n            response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=self.timeout)\n            response.raise_for_status()\n            data = response.json()\n            submissions = self._parse_response(data)\n            self.logger.info(f"Fetched {len(submissions)} submissions from form {form_id}.")\n            return submissions\n        except requests.RequestException as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Error fetching data from MachForm {form_id}: {e}")\n            return []\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Unexpected error parsing MachForm {form_id}: {e}")\n            return []\n\n    def _parse_response(self, data: dict) -> List[Dict]:\n        """\n        Parse the MachForm API response into a list of submission dicts.\n\n        Args:\n            data (dict): Raw response data from MachForm API.\n\n        Returns:\n            List[Dict]: List of parsed submissions.\n        """\n        # MachForm API responses may vary; adjust parsing as needed.\n        # Assume \'entries\' is the key for submissions.\n        if "entries" in data and isinstance(data["entries"], list):\n            return data["entries"]\n        elif isinstance(data, list):\n            return data\n        else:\n            # Try to find the first list in the response\n            for v in data.values():\n                if isinstance(v, list):\n                    return v\n        return []\n\n    def fetch_all_forms(self, form_ids: api_keys (Dict[str, str]): Mapping of form_id to API keys for authentication.\n            timeout (int): Timeout for HTTP requests in seconds.\n        """\n        self.base_urls = base_urls\n        self.api_keys = api_keys\n        self.timeout = timeout\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("MachFormClient")\n\n    def fetch_form_data(self, form_id: str) -> List[Dict]:\n        """\n        Fetch and parse data from a MachForm.\n\n        Args:\n            form_id (str): The MachForm ID to fetch data from.\n\n        Returns:\n            List[Dict]: List of submission data dictionaries.\n        """\n        if form_id not in self.base_urls or form_id not in self.api_keys:\n            self.logger.error(f"Form ID \'{form_id}\' not configured.")\n            raise ValueError(f"Form ID \'{form_id}\' not configured.")\n\n        url = self.base_urls[form_id]\n        api_key = self.api_keys[form_id]\n\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}",\n            "Accept": "application/json"\n        }\n\n        try:\n            response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=self.timeout)\n            response.raise_for_status()\n            data = response.json()\n            submissions = self._parse_response(data)\n            self.logger.info(f"Fetched {len(submissions)} submissions from form            List[Dict]: List of parsed submissions.\n        """\n        # MachForm API responses may vary; adjust parsing as needed.\n        # Assume \'entries\' is the key for submissions.\n        if "entries" in data and isinstance(data["entries"], list):\n            return data["entries"]\n        elif isinstance(data, list):\n            return data\n        else:\n            # Try to find Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/student_evaluation_automation/openai_client.py has been successfully created, with content:
import os
import logging
from typing import Dict
import openai

class OpenAIClient:
    """
    Client to generate personalized academic pathways using the OpenAI API.
    """

    def __init__(self, api_key: str, model: str = "gpt-4", timeout: int = 30):
        """
        Initialize the OpenAIClient.

        Args:
            api_key (str): OpenAI API key.
            model (str): OpenAI model to use.
            timeout (int): Timeout for API requests in seconds.
        """
        self.api_key = api_key
        self.model = model
        self.timeout = timeout
        self.logger = logging.getLogger("OpenAIClient")
        openai.api_key = self.api_key

    def generate_pathway(self, student_data: Dict) -> str:
        """
        Generate a personalized academic pathway using the OpenAI API.

        Args:
            student_data (Dict): Dictionary containing student information and MachForm data.

        Returns:
            str: Generated academic pathway text.
        """
        prompt = self._build_prompt(student_data)
        try:
            response = openai.ChatCompletion.create(
                model=self.model,
                messages=[
                    {"role": "system", "content": "You are an expert academic advisor. Based on the student's background and goals, generate a personalized academic pathway including recommended courses, milestones, and next steps. Be clear, actionable, and professional."},
                    {"role": "user", "content": prompt}
                ],
                max_tokens=800,
                temperature=0.7,
                timeout=self.timeout
            )
            pathway = response.choices[0].message['content'].strip()
            self.logger.info("Generated academic pathway for student: %s", student_data.get("student_id", "unknown"))
            return pathway
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"OpenAI API error for student {student_data.get('student_id', 'unknown')}: {e}")
            return "We were unable to generate a personalized academic pathway at this time. Please contact admissions for assistance."

    def _build_prompt(self, student_data: Dict) -> str:
        """
        Build a prompt for the OpenAI API based on student data.

        Args:
            student_data (Dict): Dictionary containing student information and MachForm data.

        Returns:
            str: Constructed prompt string.
        """
        name = student_data.get("name", "Student")
        background = student_data.get("machform_data", {})
        academic_goals = background.get("form1", {}).get("academic_goals", "")
        interests = background.get("form2", {}).get("interests", "")
        prior_courses = background.get("form3", {}).get("prior_courses", "")

        prompt = (
            f"Student Name: {name}\n"
            f"Academic Goals: {academic_goals}\n"
            f"Interests: {interests}\n"
            f"Prior Courses: {prior_courses}\n"
            "Please generate a personalized academic pathway for this student, including recommended courses, milestones, and next steps. "
            "Format the response in clear, actionable steps."
        )
        return prompt

Technical Specifications\\n061|\\n062|### Requirements Analysis\\n063|- Integration with MachForms: Must securely fetch and parse data from three distinct MachForms.\\n064|- Google Sheets Sync: Must aggregate and update student data in a master Google Sheet in real time.\\n065|- OpenAI API: Must generate personalized academic pathways based on student data and program requirements.\\n066|- Google Docs Automation: Must fill a branded template with pathway details and export as PDF.\\n067|- Email Delivery: Must send the PDF to the student’s email address automatically.\\n068|- Salesforce Storage: Must upload the PDF and student record to Salesforce, linking to the correct contact.\\n069|- Admin Tracking Interface: Must provide a dashboard for admins to monitor evaluation status, document delivery, and Salesforce sync.\\n070|- Security: Must comply with FERPA and institutional data privacy standards.\\n071|\\n072|### Requirements Pool\\n073|- **P0 (Must-have):**\\n074|  - Optional\nimport logging\n\n\nclass MachFormClient:\n    """\n    Client to fetch and parse data from MachForms.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, base_urls: Dict[str, str], api_keys: Dict[str, str], timeout: int = 10):\n        """\n        Initialize the MachFormClient.\n\n        Args:\n            base_urls (Dict[str, str]): Mapping of form_id to MachForm API endpoint URLs.\n            api_keys (Dict[str, str]): Mapping of form_id to API keys for authentication.\n            timeout (int): Timeout for HTTP requests in seconds.\n        """\n        self.base_urls = base_urls\n        self.api_keys = api_keys\n        self.timeout = timeout\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("MachFormClient")\n\n    def fetch_form_data(self, form_id: str) -> List[Dict]:\n        """\n        Fetch and parse data from a MachForm.\n\n        Args:\n            form_id (str): The MachForm ID to fetch data from.\n\n        Returns:\n            List[Dict]: List of submission data dictionaries.\n        """\n        if form_id not in self.base_urls or form_id not in self.api_keys:\n            self.logger.error(f"Form ID \'{form_id}\' not configured.")\n            raise ValueError(f"Form ID \'{form_id}\' not configured.")\n\n        url = self.base_urls[form_id]\n        api_key = self.api_keys[form_id]\n\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}",\n            "Accept": "application/json"\n        }\n\n        try:\n            response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=self.timeout)\n            response.raise_for_status()\n            data = response.json()\n            submissions = self._parse_response(data)\n            self.logger.info(f"Fetched {len(submissions)} submissions from form {form_id}.")\n            return submissions\n        except requests.RequestException as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Error fetching data from MachForm {form_id}: {e}")\n            return []\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Unexpected error parsing MachForm {form_id}: {e}")\n            return []\n\n    def _parse_response(self, data: dict) -> List[Dict]:\n        """\n        Parse the MachForm API response into a list of submission dicts.\n\n        Args:\n            data (dict): Raw response data from MachForm API.\n\n        Returns:\n            List[Dict]: List of parsed submissions.\n        """\n        # MachForm API responses may vary; adjust parsing as needed.\n        # Assume \'entries\' is the key for submissions.\n        if "entries" in data and isinstance(data["entries"], list):\n            return data["entries"]\n        elif isinstance(data, list):\n            return data\n        else:\n            # Try to find the first list in the response\n            for v in data.values():\n                if isinstance(v, list):\n                    return v\n        return []\n\n    def fetch_all_forms(self, form_ids: Based on the student\'s background and goals, generate a personalized academic pathway including recommended courses, milestones, and next steps. Be clear, actionable, and professional."},\n                    {"role": "user", "content": prompt}\n                ],\n                max_tokens=800,\n                temperature=0.7,\n                timeout=self.timeout\n            )\n            pathway = response.choices[0].message[\'content\'].strip()\n            self.logger.info("Generated academic pathway for student: %s", student_data.get("student_id", "unknown"))\n            return pathway\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"OpenAI API error for student {student_data.get(\'student_id\', \'unknown\')}: {e}")\n            return "We were unable to generate a personalized academic pathway at this time. timeout: int = 10):\n        """\n        Initialize the MachFormClient.\n\n        Args:\n            base_urls (Dict[str, str]): Mapping of form_id to MachForm API endpoint URLs.\n            api_keys (Dict[str, str]): Mapping of form_id to API keys for authentication.\n            timeout (int): Timeout for HTTP requests in seconds.\n        """\n        self.base_urls = base_urls\n        self.api_keys = api_keys\n        self.timeout = timeout\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("MachFormClient")\n\n    def fetch_form_data(self, form_id: str) -> List[Dict]:\n        """\n        Fetch and parse data from a MachForm.\n\n        Args:\n            form_id (str): The MachForm ID to fetch data from.\n\n        Returns:\n            List[Dict]: List of submission data dictionaries.\n        """\n        if form_id not in self.base_urls or form_id not in self.api_keys:\n            self.logger.error(f"Form ID \'{form_id}\' not configured.")\n            raise ValueError(f"Form ID \'{form_id}\' not configured.")\n\n        url = self.base_urls[form_id]\n        api_key = self.api_keys[form_id]\n\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}",\n            "Accept": "application/json"\n        }\n\n        try:\n            response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=self.timeout)\n            response.raise_for_status()\n            data = response.json()\n            submissions = self._parse_response(data)\n            self.logger.info(f"Fetched {len(submissions)} submissions from form {form_id}.")\n            return submissions\n        except requests.RequestException as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Error fetching data from MachForm {form_id}: {e}")\n            return List[Dict]:\n        """\n        Parse the MachForm API response into a list of submission dicts.\n\n        Args:\n            data (dict): Raw response data from MachForm API.\n\n        Returns:\n            List[Dict]: List of parsed submissions.\n        """\n        # MachForm API responses may vary; adjust parsing as needed.\n        # Assume \'entries\' is the key for submissions.\n        if "entries" in data and isinstance(data["entries"], list):\n            return data["entries"]\n        elif isinstance(data, list):\n            return data\n        else:\n            # Try to find the first list in the response\n            for v in data.values():\n                if isinstance(v, list):\n                    return v\n        return []\n\n    def fetch_all_forms(self, form_ids: form IDs to fetch. Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/student_evaluation_automation/docs_client.py has been successfully created, with content:
import os
import logging
from typing import Optional
from models import StudentRecord

from google.oauth2.service_account import Credentials
from googleapiclient.discovery import build
from googleapiclient.errors import HttpError
from googleapiclient.http import MediaFileUpload

class DocsClient:
    """
    Handles filling a branded Google Docs template with pathway details and exporting as PDF.
    """

    def __init__(self, creds_path: str, template_doc_id: str, output_folder_id: str):
        """
        Initialize the DocsClient.

        Args:
            creds_path (str): Path to the Google service account credentials JSON file.
            template_doc_id (str): Google Docs template document ID.
            output_folder_id (str): Google Drive folder ID to store generated docs.
        """
        self.logger = logging.getLogger("DocsClient")
        self.creds_path = creds_path
        self.template_doc_id = template_doc_id
        self.output_folder_id = output_folder_id
        self.scopes = [
            "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/documents",
            "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive"
        ]
        self.creds = Credentials.from_service_account_file(self.creds_path, scopes=self.scopes)
        self.docs_service = build('docs', 'v1', credentials=self.creds)
        self.drive_service = build('drive', 'v3', credentials=self.creds)

    def fill_template(self, student: StudentRecord) -> Optional[str]:
        """
        Fill the branded Google Docs template with student pathway details.

        Args:
            student (StudentRecord): The student record to fill into the template.

        Returns:
            str: The new Google Doc ID.
        """
        try:
            # 1. Copy the template
            copy_title = f"{student.name} - Academic Pathway"
            copied_file = self.drive_service.files().copy(
                fileId=self.template_doc_id,
                body={
                    'name': copy_title,
                    'parents': [self.output_folder_id]
                }
            ).execute()
            new_doc_id = copied_file['id']
            self.logger.info(f"Copied template for student {student.student_id} to doc {new_doc_id}")

            # 2. Prepare replacements
            replacements = {
                "{{STUDENT_NAME}}": student.name,
                "{{STUDENT_ID}}": student.student_id,
                "{{STUDENT_EMAIL}}": student.email,
                "{{ACADEMIC_PATHWAY}}": student.academic_pathway or "",
                "{{STATUS}}": student.status,
                # Add more fields as needed from machform_data
            }
            # Flatten machform_data for replacement
            for form_key, form_data in student.machform_data.items():
                for k, v in form_data.items():
                    replacements[f"{{{{{form_key.upper()}_{k.upper()}}}}}"] = str(v)

            # 3. Replace placeholders in the document
            requests = []
            for placeholder, value in replacements.items():
                requests.append({
                    "replaceAllText": {
                        "containsText": {
                            "text": placeholder,
                            "matchCase": True
                        },
                        "replaceText": value
                    }
                })
            self.docs_service.documents().batchUpdate(
                documentId=new_doc_id,
                body={"requests": requests}
            ).execute()
            self.logger.info(f"Filled template for student {student.student_id} in doc {new_doc_id}")
            return new_doc_id
        except HttpError as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Google Docs API error for student {student.student_id}: {e}")
            return None
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Unexpected error filling template for student {student.student_id}: {e}")
            return None

    def export_pdf(self, doc_id: str) -> Optional[str]:
        """
        Export the filled Google Doc as a PDF and download it locally.

        Args:
            doc_id (str): The Google Doc ID to export.

        Returns:
            str: Path to the downloaded PDF file.
        """
        try:
            # 1. Export the document as PDF from Google Drive
            request = self.drive_service.files().export_media(
                fileId=doc_id,
                mimeType='application/pdf'
            )
            pdf_filename = f"{doc_id}.pdf"
            pdf_path = os.path.join("templates", pdf_filename)
            with open(pdf_path, "wb") as f:
                f.write(request.execute())
            self.logger.info(f"Exported doc {doc_id} as PDF to {pdf_path}")
            return pdf_path
        except HttpError as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Google Drive API error exporting PDF for doc {doc_id}: {e}")
            return None
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Unexpected error exporting PDF for doc {doc_id}: {e}")
            return None

Based on the student\'s background and goals, generate a personalized academic pathway including recommended courses, milestones, and next steps. Be clear, actionable, and professional."},\n                    {"role": "user", "content": prompt}\n                ],\n                max_tokens=800,\n                temperature=0.7,\n                timeout=self.timeout\n            )\n            pathway = response.choices[0].message[\'content\'].strip()\n            self.logger.info("Generated academic pathway for student: %s", student_data.get("student_id", "unknown"))\n            return pathway\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"OpenAI API error for student {student_data.get(\'student_id\', \'unknown\')}: {e}")\n            return "We were unable to generate a personalized academic pathway at this time. googleapiclient.http import MediaFileUpload\n\nclass DocsClient:\n    """\n    Handles filling a branded Google Docs template with pathway details and exporting as PDF.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, creds_path: str, template_doc_id: str, output_folder_id: str):\n        """\n        Initialize the DocsClient.\n\n        Args:\n            creds_path (str): Path to the Google service account credentials JSON file.\n            template_doc_id (str): Google Docs template document ID.\n            output_folder_id (str): Google Drive folder ID to store generated docs.\n        """\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("DocsClient")\n        self.creds_path = creds_path\n        self.template_doc_id = template_doc_id\n        self.output_folder_id = output_folder_id\n        self.scopes = [\n            "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/documents",\n            "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive"\n        ]\n        self.creds = Credentials.from_service_account_file(self.creds_path, scopes=self.scopes)\n        self.docs_service = build(\'docs\', \'v1\', credentials=self.creds)\n        self.drive_service = build(\'drive\', \'v3\', credentials=self.creds)\n\n    def fill_template(self, student: StudentRecord) -> Optional[str]:\n        """\n        Fill the branded Google Docs template with student pathway details.\n\n        Args:\n            student (StudentRecord): The student record to fill into the template.\n\n        Returns:\n            str: The new Google Doc ID.\n        """\n        try:\n            # 1. Copy the template\n            copy_title = f"{student.name} - Academic Pathway"\n            copied_file = self.drive_service.files().copy(\n                fileId=self.template_doc_id,\n                body={\n                    \'name\': copy_title,\n                    \'parents\': [self.output_folder_id]\n                }\n            ).execute()\n            new_doc_id = copied_file[\'id\']\n            self.logger.info(f"Copied template for student {student.student_id} to doc {new_doc_id}")\n\n            # 2. Prepare replacements\n            replacements = {\n                "{{STUDENT_NAME}}": student.name,\n                "{{STUDENT_ID}}": student.student_id,\n                "{{STUDENT_EMAIL}}": student.email,\n                "{{ACADEMIC_PATHWAY}}": student.academic_pathway or "",\n                "{{STATUS}}": student.status,\n                # Add more fields as needed from machform_data\n            }\n            # Flatten machform_data for replacement\n            for form_key, form_data in student.machform_data.items():\n                for k, v in form_data.items():\n                    replacements[f"{{{{{form_key.upper()}_{k.upper()}}}}}"] = str(v)\n\n            # 3. Replace placeholders in the document\n            requests = []\n            for placeholder, value in replacements.items():\n                requests.append({\n                    "replaceAllText": {\n                        "containsText": {\n                            "text": placeholder,\n                            "matchCase": True\n                        },\n                        "replaceText": value\n                    }\n                })\n            self.docs_service.documents().batchUpdate(\n                documentId=new_doc_id,\n                body={"requests": requests}\n            ).execute()\n            self.logger.info(f"Filled template for student {student.student_id} in doc {new_doc_id}")\n            return new_doc_id\n        except HttpError as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Google Docs API error for student {student.student_id}: {e}")\n            return None\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Unexpected error filling template for student {student.student_id}: {e}")\n            return None\n\n    def export_pdf(self, doc_id: str) -> Optional[str]:\n        """\n        Export the filled Google Doc as a PDF and download it locally.\n\n        Args:\n            doc_id (str): The Google Doc ID to export.\n\n        Returns:\n            str: Path to the downloaded PDF file.\n        """\n        try:\n            # 1. Export the document as PDF from Google Drive\n            request = self.drive_service.files().export_media(\n                fileId=doc_id,\n                mimeType=\'application/pdf\'\n            )\n            pdf_filename = f"{doc_id}.pdf"\n            pdf_path = os.path.join("templates", pdf_filename)\n            with open(pdf_path, "wb") as f:\n                f.write(request.execute())\n            self.logger.info(f"Exported doc {doc_id} as PDF to {pdf_path}")\n            return pdf_path\n        except HttpError as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Google Drive API error exporting PDF for doc {doc_id}: {e}")\n            return None\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Unexpected error exporting PDF for doc {doc_id}: {e}")\n            return None\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Based on the student\'s background and goals, generate a personalized academic pathway including recommended courses, milestones, and next steps. Be clear, actionable, and professional."},\n                    {"role": "user", "content": prompt}\n                ],\n                max_tokens=800,\n                temperature=0.7,\n                timeout=self.timeout\n            )\n            pathway = response.choices[0].message[\'content\'].strip()\n            self.logger.info("Generated academic pathway for student: %s", student_data.get("student_id", "unknown"))\n            return pathway\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"OpenAI API error for student {student_data.get(\'student_id\', \'unknown\')}: {e}")\n            return "We were unable to generate a personalized academic pathway at this time. \'v3\', credentials=self.creds)\n\n    def fill_template(self, student: StudentRecord) -> Optional[str]:\n        """\n        Fill the branded Google Docs template with student pathway details.\n\n        Args:\n            student (StudentRecord): The student record to fill into the template.\n\n        Returns:\n            str: The new Google Doc ID.\n        """\n        try:\n            # 1. Copy the template\n            copy_title = f"{student.name} - Academic Pathway"\n            copied_file = self.drive_service.files().copy(\n                fileId=self.template_doc_id,\n                body={\n                    \'name\': copy_title,\n                    \'parents\': [self.output_folder_id]\n                }\n            ).execute()\n            new_doc_id = copied_file[\'id\']\n            self.logger.info(f"Copied template for student {student.student_id} to doc {new_doc_id}")\n\n            # 2. Prepare replacements\n            replacements = {\n                "{{STUDENT_NAME}}": student.name,\n                "{{STUDENT_ID}}": student.student_id,\n                "{{STUDENT_EMAIL}}": student.email,\n                "{{ACADEMIC_PATHWAY}}": student.academic_pathway or "",\n                "{{STATUS}}": student.status,\n                # Add more fields as needed from machform_data\n            }\n            # Flatten machform_data for replacement\n            for form_key, form_data in student.machform_data.items():\n                for k, v in form_data.items():\n                    replacements[f"{{{{{form_key.upper()}_{k.upper()}}}}}"] = str(v)\n\n            # 3. Replace placeholders in the document\n            requests = []\n            for placeholder, value in replacements.items():\n                requests.append({\n                    "replaceAllText": {\n                        "containsText": {\n                            "text": placeholder,\n                            "matchCase": True\n                        },\n                        "replaceText": value\n                    }\n                })\n            self.docs_service.documents().batchUpdate(\n                documentId=new_doc_id,\n                body={"requests": requests}\n            ).execute()\n            self.logger.info(f"Filled template for student {student.student_id} in doc {new_doc_id}")\n            return new_doc_id\n        except HttpError as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Google Docs API error for student {student.student_id}: {e}")\n            return None\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Unexpected error filling template for student {student.student_id}: {e}")\n            return None\n\n    def export_pdf(self, doc_id: str) -> Optional[str]:\n        """\n        Export the filled Google Doc as a PDF and download it locally.\n\n        Args:\n            doc_id (str): The Google Doc ID to export.\n\n        Returns:\n            str: Path to the downloaded PDF file.\n        """\n        try:\n            # 1. Export the document as PDF from Google Drive\n            request = self.drive_service.files().export_media(\n                fileId=doc_id,\n                mimeType=\'application/pdf\'\n            )\n            pdf_filename = f"{doc_id}.pdf"\n            pdf_path = os.path.join("templates", pdf_filename)\n            with open(pdf_path, "wb") as f:\n                f.write(request.execute())\n            self.logger.info(f"Exported doc {doc_id} as PDF to {pdf_path}")\n            return pdf_path\n        except HttpError as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Google Drive API error exporting PDF for doc {doc_id}: {e}")\n            return None\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Unexpected error exporting PDF for doc {doc_id}: {e}")\n            return None\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. import os
import logging
import pdfkit

class PDFExporter:
    """
    Handles PDF export from a document path (HTML or DOCX converted to HTML).
    Uses pdfkit to generate PDF if Google Docs export is insufficient.
    """

    def __init__(self, wkhtmltopdf_path: str = None):
        """
        Initialize the PDFExporter.

        Args:
            wkhtmltopdf_path (str, optional): Path to the wkhtmltopdf executable. If None, uses default.
        """
        self.logger = logging.getLogger("PDFExporter")
        self.wkhtmltopdf_path = wkhtmltopdf_path
        self.config = pdfkit.configuration(wkhtmltopdf=wkhtmltopdf_path) if wkhtmltopdf_path else None

    def export(self, doc_path: str) -> str:
        """
        Export the given document (HTML file) as a PDF.

        Args:
            doc_path (str): Path to the HTML document to convert.

        Returns:
            str: Path to the generated PDF file.
        """
        if not os.path.exists(doc_path):
            self.logger.error(f"Document path does not exist: {doc_path}")
            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Document path does not exist: {doc_path}")

        # Determine output PDF path
        base_name = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(doc_path))[0]
        pdf_path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(doc_path), f"{base_name}.pdf")

        try:
            # Convert HTML to PDF
            pdfkit.from_file(doc_path, pdf_path, configuration=self.config)
            self.logger.info(f"Exported PDF: {pdf_path}")
            return pdf_path
        except Exception as e:
            self.logger.error(f"Failed to export PDF from {doc_path}: {e}")
            raise

3. Technical Specifications\\n061|\\n062|### Requirements Analysis\\n063|- Integration with MachForms: Must securely fetch and parse data from three distinct MachForms.\\n064|- Google Sheets Sync: Must aggregate and update student data in a master Google Sheet in real time.\\n065|- OpenAI API: Must generate personalized academic pathways based on student data and program requirements.\\n066|- Google Docs Automation: Must fill a branded template with pathway details and export as PDF.\\n067|- Email Delivery: Must send the PDF to the student’s email address automatically.\\n068|- Salesforce Storage: Must upload the PDF and student record to Salesforce, linking to the correct contact.\\n069|- Admin Tracking Interface: Must provide a dashboard for admins to monitor evaluation status, document delivery, and Salesforce sync.\\n070|- Security: Must comply with FERPA and institutional data privacy standards.\\n071|\\n072|### Requirements Pool\\n073|- **P0 (Must-have):**\\n074|  - MachForms integration\\n075|  - Google Sheets master record\\n076|  - OpenAI API pathway generation\\n077|  - Google Docs template automation\\n078|  - PDF export and email delivery\\n079|  - Salesforce record storage\\n080|  - Admin tracking dashboard\\n081|- **P1 (Should-have):**\\n082|  - Error handling and retry logic\\n083|  - Audit logs for all actions\\n084|  - Customizable email templates\\n085|- **P2 (Nice-to-have):**\\n086|  - Mobile-friendly admin interface\\n087|  - Analytics on student pathway trends\\n088|  - Multi-language support\\n089|\\n090|### UI Design Draft\\n091|- **Admin Dashboard:**\\n092|  - Student list with status indicators (data received, pathway generated, PDF sent, Salesforce stored)\\n093|  - Search and filter by name, status, date\\n094|  - Detail view for each student: form data, pathway, document links\\n095|  - Manual override and re-send options\\n096|- **Student Email:**\\n097|  - Branded message with attached PDF\\n098|  - Link to contact admissions for questions\\n099|\\n100|### Open Questions\\n101|- What is the expected volume of incoming students per cycle?\\n102|- Are there specific data fields or formats required from MachForms?\\n103|- Should admins be able to edit pathways before sending?\\n104|- What branding elements are required for the Google Docs template?\\n105|- What Salesforce object(s) should the PDF be attached to?\\n\'\n\nCommand Editor.read executed: file_path=\'/home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/student_evaluation_automation/docs/system_design.md\' block_content=\'001|## Implementation approach\\n002|\\n003|We will use Python as the main language, leveraging open-source libraries for integration:\\n004|- MachForms: Data fetched via HTTP requests (assuming API or webhooks; otherwise, web scraping).\\n005|- Google Sheets: Use `gspread` or `google-api-python-client` for Sheets API.\\n006|- OpenAI API: Use `openai` Python SDK.\\n007|- Google Docs & PDF: Use `google-api-python-client` for Docs, and `pdfkit` or Google Docs export for PDF.\\n008|- Email: Use `smtplib` or `sendgrid` for sending emails.\\n009|- Salesforce: Use `simple-salesforce` for API integration.\\n010|- Admin Tracking Interface: Use `Flask` or `FastAPI` for a web dashboard, with `Bootstrap` for UI.\\n011|- Security: Use OAuth2 for Google and Salesforce, environment variables for secrets, and logging for audit.\\n012|\\n013|## File list\\n014|\\n015|- main.py\\n016|- config.py\\n017|- machform_client.py\\n018|- sheets_client.py\\n019|- openai_client.py\\n020|- docs_client.py\\n021|- pdf_exporter.py\\n022|- email_sender.py\\n023|- salesforce_client.py\\n024|- admin_dashboard.py\\n025|- models.py\\n026|- utils.py\\n027|- requirements.txt\\n028|- templates/\\n029|    - Optional\nimport logging\n\n\nclass MachFormClient:\n    """\n    Client to fetch and parse data from MachForms.\n    """\n\n    def __init__(self, base_urls: Dict[str, str], api_keys: Dict[str, str], timeout: int = 10):\n        """\n        Initialize the MachFormClient.\n\n        Args:\n            base_urls (Dict[str, str]): Mapping of form_id to MachForm API endpoint URLs.\n            api_keys (Dict[str, str]): Mapping of form_id to API keys for authentication.\n            timeout (int): Timeout for HTTP requests in seconds.\n        """\n        self.base_urls = base_urls\n        self.api_keys = api_keys\n        self.timeout = timeout\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("MachFormClient")\n\n    def fetch_form_data(self, form_id: str) -> List[Dict]:\n        """\n        Fetch and parse data from a MachForm.\n\n        Args:\n            form_id (str): The MachForm ID to fetch data from.\n\n        Returns:\n            List[Dict]: List of submission data dictionaries.\n        """\n        if form_id not in self.base_urls or form_id not in self.api_keys:\n            self.logger.error(f"Form ID \'{form_id}\' not configured.")\n            raise ValueError(f"Form ID \'{form_id}\' not configured.")\n\n        url = self.base_urls[form_id]\n        api_key = self.api_keys[form_id]\n\n        headers = {\n            "Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}",\n            "Accept": "application/json"\n        }\n\n        try:\n            response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=self.timeout)\n            response.raise_for_status()\n            data = response.json()\n            submissions = self._parse_response(data)\n            self.logger.info(f"Fetched {len(submissions)} submissions from form            List[Dict]: List of parsed submissions.\n        """\n        # MachForm API responses may vary; adjust parsing as needed.\n        # Assume \'entries\' is the key for submissions.\n the first list in the response\n            for v in data.values():\n                if isinstance(v, list):\n                    return v\n        return []\n\n    def fetch_all_forms(self, form_ids: \'v3\', credentials=self.creds)\n\n    def fill_template(self, student: StudentRecord) -> Optional[str]:\n        """\n        Fill the branded Google Docs template with student pathway details.\n\n        Args:\n            student (StudentRecord): The student record to fill into the template.\n\n        Returns:\n            str: The new Google Doc ID.\n        """\n        try:\n            # 1. Copy the template\n            copy_title = f"{student.name} - Academic Pathway"\n            copied_file = self.drive_service.files().copy(\n                fileId=self.template_doc_id,\n                body={\n                    \'name\': copy_title,\n                    \'parents\': [self.output_folder_id]\n                }\n            ).execute()\n            new_doc_id = copied_file[\'id\']\n            self.logger.info(f"Copied template for student {student.student_id} to doc {new_doc_id}")\n\n            # 2. If None, uses default.\n        """\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger("PDFExporter")\n        self.wkhtmltopdf_path = wkhtmltopdf_path\n        self.config = pdfkit.configuration(wkhtmltopdf=wkhtmltopdf_path) if wkhtmltopdf_path else None\n\n    def export(self, doc_path: str) -> str:\n        """\n        Export the given document (HTML file) as a PDF.\n\n        Args:\n            doc_path (str): Path to the HTML document to convert.\n\n        Returns:\n            str: Path to the generated PDF file.\n        """\n        if not os.path.exists(doc_path):\n            self.logger.error(f"Document path does not exist: {doc_path}")\n            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Document path does not exist: {doc_path}")\n\n        # Determine output PDF path\n        base_name = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(doc_path))[0]\n        pdf_path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(doc_path), f"{base_name}.pdf")\n\n        try:\n            # Convert HTML to PDF\n            pdfkit.from_file(doc_path, pdf_path, configuration=self.config)\n            self.logger.info(f"Exported PDF: {pdf_path}")\n            return pdf_path\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"Failed to export PDF from {doc_path}: {e}")\n            raise\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. export(doc_path)\\n119|    PE-->>SR: return pdf_path\\n120|    SR->>ES: send_email(to, subject, body, attachment)\\n121|    ES-->>SR: email sent\\n122|    SR->>SF: upload_document(student, Based on the student\'s background and goals, generate a personalized academic pathway including recommended courses, milestones, and next steps. Be clear, actionable, and professional."},\n                    {"role": "user", "content": prompt}\n                ],\n                max_tokens=800,\n                temperature=0.7,\n                timeout=self.timeout\n            )\n            pathway = response.choices[0].message[\'content\'].strip()\n            self.logger.info("Generated academic pathway for student: %s", student_data.get("student_id", "unknown"))\n            return pathway\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.logger.error(f"OpenAI API error for student {student_data.get(\'student_id\', \'unknown\')}: {e}")\n            return "We were unable to generate a personalized academic pathway at this time.
